items.title: Final/OT: Sparks 111, Mercury 102. Kelsey Plum will get the headlines, and rightfully so, but Cameron Brink, Nneka Ogwumike, and Rae Burrell all had lasting imprints on this one, as did the whole team. Plum scored a franchise-record-tying 43 points. Sparks are 7-6. What a game.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Final/OT: Sparks 111, Mercury 102. Kelsey Plum will get the headlines, and rightfully so, but Cameron Brink, Nneka Ogwumike, and Rae Burrell all had lasting imprints on this one, as did the whole team. Plum scored a franchise-record-tying 43 points. Sparks are 7-6. What a game.

items.title: The Spurs were -8 in 16 minutes in the clutch in the finals (game within 5 over the last 4 minutes) and were -13.7 points-per-100-possessions. The problem, of course, was their offense. They averaged just 89.2 points/100 poss.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: The Spurs were -8 in 16 minutes in the clutch in the finals (game within 5 over the last 4 minutes) and were -13.7 points-per-100-possessions. The problem, of course, was their offense. They averaged just 89.2 points/100 poss.

items.title: The most KAT thing of all time — he was holding up the walkway off the court because he wanted to share the trophy with Knicks fans, so he stopped and leaned into the stands to let a bunch of them touch it. They reacted like it was the best moment of their lives.
items.author: Fred Katz
items.body_excerpt: The most KAT thing of all time — he was holding up the walkway off the court because he wanted to share the trophy with Knicks fans, so he stopped and leaned into the stands to let a bunch of them touch it. They reacted like it was the best moment of their lives.

items.title: Cameron Brink has been trusted quite a bit here, as she started the overtime period over Dearica Hamby, and it’s a decision that has paid off by Lynne Roberts. Brink has changed this overtime defensively.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Cameron Brink has been trusted quite a bit here, as she started the overtime period over Dearica Hamby, and it’s a decision that has paid off by Lynne Roberts. Brink has changed this overtime defensively.

items.title: NBA franchises that have waited 54+ years in the NBA for a title entering the 2026-27 season:
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
NBA franchises that have waited 54+ years in the NBA for a title entering the 2026-27 season:

Rochester to Sacramento Kings: 75 seasons w/o title

St Louis to Atlanta Hawks: 68 seasons w/o title

Phoenix Suns: 58 seasons w/o title

Buffalo to LA Clippers: 56 seasons w/o title

items.title: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of falling short in quest for title: “I don't think anybody other than the people in that room expected us to be here. So there was a lot of good in that and there's a lot of pain in what just happened and both things can be true
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of falling short in quest for title: “I don't think anybody other than the people in that room expected us to be here. So there was a lot of good in that and there's a lot of pain in what just happened and both things can be true.”

items.title: I'm sure I'm not the first to point this out, but this year was the exact reverse of '99. In '99, Spurs won Games 1, 2, 4 and 5 and clinched on the road. In '26, Knicks won Games 1, 2, 4 and 5 and clinched on the road.
items.author: Sports Media Watch
items.body_excerpt: I'm sure I'm not the first to point this out, but this year was the exact reverse of '99. In '99, Spurs won Games 1, 2, 4 and 5 and clinched on the road. In '26, Knicks won Games 1, 2, 4 and 5 and clinched on the road.
